name: Inverse Finance Anchor Oracle Manipulation
tldr: Inverse Finance's Anchor lending market lost ~$15.6M when an attacker pumped the INV/WETH SushiSwap TWAP feed that Anchor's Keep3r oracle read, then borrowed ETH, WBTC, YFI, and DOLA against a tiny amount of INV collateral priced at the manipulated rate.
coreContradiction: Anchor trusted a Keep3r TWAP rooted in a thinly traded SushiSwap INV/WETH pool, so collateral value was decided by the same pool a single actor could move with ~500 ETH of swap volume.

attackSteps.label: Fund from Tornado
attackSteps.detail: Attacker drew 901 ETH from Tornado Cash and spread 1.5 ETH across 241 fresh addresses via Disperse to pay gas while spamming blocks around the oracle update.

attackSteps.label: Pump INV price via SushiSwap
attackSteps.detail: Attacker EOA 0x8B4C... ran tx 0x20a6dc... through the exploit contract, swapping WETH->INV through the shallow SushiSwap INV/WETH pair (and the INV/DOLA route) to push INV's price ~50x on the Keep3r TWAP feed.

attackSteps.label: Spam blocks to freeze the TWAP
attackSteps.detail: Gas-bribed spam transactions ensured the inflated SushiSwap price was observed by Keep3r's TWAP update before arbitrage could revert it, so Anchor read the manipulated price.

attackSteps.label: Deposit INV, borrow everything
attackSteps.detail: Second EOA 0x117C... called mint() on the attacker contract (tx 0x600373...) which deposited ~1,746 INV into anINV (xINV), entered the markets, and borrowed 1,588 ETH, 94 WBTC, 39.37 YFI, and 3,999,669 DOLA from anETH/anWBTC/anYFI/anDOLA.

attackSteps.label: Exit through Tornado
attackSteps.detail: ~$15.6M of borrowed assets were swapped to ETH and funneled back through Tornado Cash while the INV collateral was left behind to be liquidated at a fraction of the borrow value.

fundFlow.note: proceeds consolidated to Exploiter 1 EOA before Tornado Cash
